name: 05 What is MANUAL EXPOSURE in photography:Guide for beginner

step.name: Set Aperture for Depth Single subject/couple close: f/1.8–f/2.8 for separation.
step.text: Set Aperture for Depth Single subject/couple close: f/1.8–f/2.8 for separation. Subjects slightly apart: f/2.8–f/4 to hold both sharp. Larger groups: start at f/2.8–f/4+ and adjust with spacing. Landscapes/architecture: typically f/8–f/11 .

step.name: Choose Shutter for Motion Static scenes: about 1/60 s (watch for shake).
step.text: Choose Shutter for Motion Static scenes: about 1/60 s (watch for shake). Slow movement (walk/dance): ~ 1/200 s . Faster action (quick dance/jog): ≥ 1/500 s to freeze. Hand-held guardrail: ~ 1/(2× focal length) ; use support/IBIS if slower.

step.name: Set ISO to Meet the Meter Use the lowest ISO that keeps your chosen aperture/...
step.text: Set ISO to Meet the Meter Use the lowest ISO that keeps your chosen aperture/shutter. Too dark → raise ISO; too bright → lower ISO to hit the meter. Prefer speed? Enable Auto ISO with a minimum shutter so the camera adjusts ISO while you lock aperture & shutter intent.
